summary: Post-market monitoring (Article 72) workflow that defines the metrics, signal sources, review cadence, and escalation paths providers operate after a high-risk AI system is on the market.
monitoring_objectives: Detect deviations from intended purpose and acceptable performance.
Surface incidents that may require notification to authorities.
Feed insights back into the risk management lifecycle.

monitoring_categories.description: Track accuracy, robustness, and fairness indicators against pre-release baselines.
Track availability, latency, and saturation of governance-critical components.
Track guardrail invocations, override events, and escalations.
Capture structured feedback and complaints from affected users.

escalation_routes.description: Engineering owner is notified and opens a remediation ticket within the operator's tracker.
Risk owner and regulatory owner review the signal and decide whether a substantial modification or incident reporting is required.
When criteria in conformity/incident-reporting-workflow.json are met, the regulatory owner triggers external notification.

boundaries: Monitoring thresholds are operator-defined; GovAI does not enforce regulator-specific thresholds automatically.
Customer-controlled deployments may apply additional monitoring obligations beyond this workflow.
